name: Cardeña and Azuel Loop
name_original: Azuel y Cardeña — Parque Natural de la Sierra de Andújar — circular
region: National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro
description: This moderate 16.8-mile touring cycling route in Sierra de Andújar offers varied terrain and beautiful Mediterranean forests.
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes around National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, low mountains, and deep river valleys. The terrain features well-preserved Mediterranean woodlands, extensive dehesas, and distinctive granite outcrops. Cyclists can expect varied elevation changes, with routes often winding through areas shaped by rivers like the Yeguas and Arenoso. Best no traffic touring cycling routes around National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Conquista y Azuel — circular desde Fuencaliente, a 31.8 miles (51.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Dehesas de Azuel Loop, a difficult 24.0 miles (38.7 km) path. This route explores the park's dehesa woodlands and offers panoramic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Cardeña and Azuel Loop, a 16.8 miles (27.1 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forests and open pastures, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro is defined by Mediterranean forests, dehesa woodlands, and granite outcrops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate day trips to challenging long-distance rides. The routes in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: There are 9 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes in the National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro featured in this guide. These routes range from moderate to difficult, offering diverse experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: The routes traverse a varied landscape of rolling hills, low mountains, and deep valleys. While many sections are paved, some routes may include unpaved portions, especially as you explore the unique Mediterranean woodlands and dehesas. Expect significant elevation changes, providing both challenging ascents and scenic descents.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic touring routes in the park?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363019169/dehesas-de-azuel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de Azuel Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 38.7 km ride, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027121082/cardena-and-azuel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña and Azuel Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 27.1 km.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the routes?
A: The park offers several points of interest. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo Bridge over the Yeguas River&lt;/a&gt;. The distinctive granite outcrops, locally known as "bolos," are also a common sight, adding to the natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year for no-traffic touring cycling in Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: The best time to visit for touring cycling is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the significant elevation changes more manageable and providing pleasant conditions to enjoy the Mediterranean woodlands and wildlife.

Q: Are there any easy no-traffic touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While all routes in this guide are rated moderate to difficult, the moderate options are more accessible for those with some cycling experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ana Luna Recreation Area – Cardeña loop from Azuel&lt;/a&gt; is a 21 km moderate route with around 300 meters of elevation gain, offering a good introduction to the park's terrain.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while cycling in the park?
A: The Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro is a crucial refuge for wildlife. You might spot various birds of prey, including golden eagles and griffon vultures, soaring overhead. Mammals like red deer, fallow deer, and wild boars are also present. The park is also one of the last strongholds for the endangered Iberian lynx, though sightings are rare.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.95 out of 5 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquility of the no-traffic routes, the stunning natural landscapes, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in the unique Mediterranean dehesa ecosystems.

Q: Are there any towns or villages near the routes where I can find amenities?
A: Yes, the village of Cardeña offers lodging options and local charm, including its church of Nuestra Señora del Carmen. The town of Montoro, a Historic-Artistic Site, also provides a wider range of amenities and cultural points of interest, though it may require a short detour from some routes.

Q: Is public transport available to access the park's cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads within the park might be limited, the towns surrounding the park, such as Montoro and Cardeña, may have bus connections. It is advisable to check local transport schedules in advance if you plan to rely on public transport to reach your starting point.

Q: Are there any challenging no-traffic routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375134449/conquista-y-azuel-circular-desde-fuencaliente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conquista y Azuel — circular desde Fuencaliente&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding ride of over 51 km with significant elevation gain, testing your endurance while providing spectacular views of the park's rugged landscapes.
tips: In the center of the town of Cardeña there is the town hall, a square with a fountain, a store selling Iberian products from the area, and some bars with the gastronomic specialties of the region. The Iberian acorn-fed ham and the fried suckling pig are true gems that you must try.
The beautiful town welcomes us with the usual slopes, and whitewashed houses before starting the ascent through the Via Crucis

In the heart of the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro natural park is this small area with rural accommodation and other facilities for rest and recreation, including the star gazebo that is part of the Starlight project.

faqs.question: What is the actual difficulty of this circular route for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: While listed as 'moderate' for touring bicycles, the terrain in the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park can be quite demanding. Expect undulating reliefs, potentially unpaved sections, gravel, and challenging gradients. Some slopes can reach 5-6%, with 'killer climbs' up to 15%. A robust touring bike, gravel bike, or even a mountain bike might be more suitable than a standard touring bicycle, and good fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the villages of Azuel or Cardeña, which are both part of the route. Parking near the Plaza de la Constitución de Cardeña or within Azuel would provide convenient access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar or the Parque Natural Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of private property.

faqs.question: Is this circular cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the natural park. However, it is crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife, especially given the presence of endangered species like the Iberian lynx. Be prepared to carry water for your dog, as sources may be limited.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making cycling challenging, while winters can be cool and sometimes wet.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through beautiful Mediterranean forests dominated by holm oaks and cork oaks, with riparian forests along riverbanks. The park is an 'oasis of peace' known for its rich biodiversity. Keep an eye out for deer, fallow deer, and wild boar. It's also a critical habitat for the endangered Iberian lynx, imperial eagle, and black vulture, though spotting them requires luck and patience. You'll also pass through traditional white villages and may get impressive views from points like the Aldea del Cerezo Starlight Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Transandalus (30%), Cañizo Fariña (18%), and Colada del Cerezo a Azuel (14%) trails. Smaller portions also overlap with Camino del Robledillo, sendero antigua carretera, Atalaya, Sta María de la Cabeza, Umbría la Vid, and Camino de Piedra Empiná.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the villages of Azuel and Cardeña, where you can find local cafes and restaurants to refuel. It's always a good idea to check their opening hours, especially outside of peak season, and carry sufficient water and snacks for the ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the potential for challenging terrain and varying conditions, pack plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and appropriate clothing layers. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is essential, especially in warmer months. A map or GPS device is highly recommended, as cell service can be spotty in some areas. Don't forget your camera for the stunning scenery!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: For a touring cyclist, the route is estimated to take around 2 hours and 35 minutes of active cycling time. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the type of bicycle you're using, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit the villages.

waypoints.name: Plaza de la Constitución de Cardeña
